title: Marriage
doc_html:
NOTE: This provision of law was included in the Unconstitutional Statutes Biennial Report to the Legislature, dated March 14, 2016.

Art. 3520. Marriage

           A. A marriage that is valid in the state where contracted, or in the state where the parties were first domiciled as husband and wife, shall be treated as a valid marriage unless to do so would violate a strong public policy of the state whose law is applicable to the particular issue under Article 3519.

           B. A purported marriage between persons of the same sex violates a strong public policy of the state of Louisiana and such a marriage contracted in another state shall not be recognized in this state for any purpose, including the assertion of any right or claim as a result of the purported marriage.

           Acts 1991, No. 923, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 1992; Acts 1999, No. 890, §1.

Art. 3520.  Marriage
A.  A marriage that is valid in the state where contracted, or in the state
where the parties were first domiciled as husband and wife, shall be treated as
a valid marriage unless to do so would violate a strong public policy of the
state whose law is applicable to the particular issue under Article 3519.
B.  A purported marriage between persons of the same sex violates a
strong public policy of the state of Louisiana and such a marriage contracted
in another state shall not be recognized in this state for any purpose, including
the assertion of any right or claim as a result of the purported marriage.
Acts 1991, No. 923, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 1992; Acts 1999, No. 890, §1.
